10	# Feature: DDx Agent Skills
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-011
13	**Status:** Implemented (root `ddx` skill with nested workflow skills present in `skills/`, `cli/internal/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, and `.ddx/skills/`; site copy pending)
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	DDx ships a single root agent-facing skill (`ddx`) that makes any
20	skills-compatible coding agent "ddx-aware" after `ddx init`. Under that root,
21	DDx may ship nested workflow skills such as `bead-breakdown/`, `replay-bead/`,
22	`compare-prompts/`, and the bead-lifecycle quality skill governed by ADR-023.
23	The root skill and nested skills are written to the
24	[agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) open standard so they work identically
25	in Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, and any other
26	harness that implements the standard.
27	
28	When the user says "do work", "review this", "what's on the queue",
29	"create a bead", or any DDx concept, the harness discovers the `ddx`
30	skill via its description, loads `SKILL.md`, and routes via an
31	explicit intent table into `reference/*.md` files that contain the
32	domain guidance.
33	
34	## Problem Statement
35	
36	Prior iterations of FEAT-011 shipped ~7 sibling skills
37	(`ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
38	`ddx-install`, `ddx-doctor`). Real-world usage exposed problems:
39	
40	- **Intent ambiguity.** Users say "do work", not "/ddx-run". A flat
41	  list of named slash commands forces the harness to guess between
42	  `/ddx-bead` vs `/ddx-run` vs `/ddx-work` from natural-language
43	  phrases.
44	- **Vocabulary drift.** Each skill redefined terms (bead, queue,
45	  harness, review) inline; wording diverged across files and from
46	  FEAT-* specs.
47	- **Workflow-opinion leakage.** `ddx-bead` mandated `helix` labels
48	  and documented `phase:*` labels — HELIX methodology opinions
49	  inside a core DDx skill.
50	- **Skill tree drift.** Two copies of most skills
51	  (`cli/internal/skills/` embedded vs top-level `/skills/`) with
52	  real content divergence.
53	- **Init gap.** `ddx init` copied only 2 of 7 embedded skills; the
54	  rest never surfaced to Claude Code unless the user re-installed
55	  manually.
56	- **Portability.** Skills used Claude-Code-only frontmatter fields
57	  (`argument-hint`) that are silently ignored by Codex and other
58	  harnesses, and reached for Claude-Code-only patterns
59	  (`context: fork`) that don't exist elsewhere.
60	
61	The consolidated design fixes each of these.
62	
63	## Architecture
64	
65	### Root skill plus nested workflow skills
66	
67	```
68	skills/ddx/
69	├── SKILL.md                # overview, vocabulary, intent-router directive
70	├── reference/
71	│   ├── beads.md            # writing execution-ready beads (best practices)
72	│   ├── work.md             # draining the queue, execute-bead, verify + close
73	│   ├── review.md           # bead-review (AC grade) + quorum code review
74	│   ├── agents.md           # harness/profile dispatch, personas
75	│   └── status.md           # queue state, doctor, health checks
76	├── evals/
77	│   └── routing.jsonl       # ~15 phrase→expected-routing fixtures
78	├── bead-breakdown/
79	│   └── SKILL.md            # workflow skill composition over ddx bead create
80	├── replay-bead/
81	│   └── SKILL.md            # workflow skill composition over ddx run / try
82	└── ...
83	```
84	
85	At harness startup, only the skill's `name` + `description` metadata
86	is pre-loaded. On activation, the harness reads `SKILL.md`. When the
87	intent router matches a phrase, the harness reads the matching
88	`reference/*.md`. Nothing else loads. This is the Anthropic
89	"Pattern 2: Domain-specific organization" pattern and matches how
90	Codex and other agentskills.io implementations progressively disclose
91	skill content.
92	
93	Nested workflow skills are independent skills stored below the root `ddx/`
94	directory so DDx can package them with the core domain vocabulary without
95	returning to the stale flat sibling model (`ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`,
96	`ddx-review`, etc.). They describe reusable workflows over DDx primitives:
97	break down an epic into beads, replay a bead under altered conditions, compare
98	prompt variants, estimate effort, run adversarial review, and perform
99	bead readiness assessment, lint/rubric scoring, and post-attempt triage. The
100	bead-lifecycle skill owns bead readiness assessment, the rubric pass inside
101	readiness, post-attempt triage, and refine guidance. They do not
102	introduce new DDx run kinds; FEAT-010's three-layer architecture remains the
103	execution substrate.
104	
105	### Portability contract
106	
107	Frontmatter is the portable-safe minimum — `name` + `description` only.
108	
109	```yaml
110	---
111	name: ddx
112	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
113	---
114	```
115	
116	No `argument-hint`, `when_to_use`, `context: fork`, `allowed-tools`,
117	`disable-model-invocation`, `user-invocable`, `paths`, `model`,
118	`effort`, `agent`, or `hooks` fields — those are Claude-Code
119	extensions that Codex and others ignore or reject. The description
120	front-loads the DDx nouns (bead, queue, execution, harness, persona,
121	spec-id) **and** the exact verb phrases users say verbatim, because
122	implicit-invocation matchers prefer substring-ish keyword matching
123	over semantic understanding.
124	
125	### Intent-router directive
126	
127	Claude does not reliably auto-chase reference links; the router in
128	`SKILL.md` is stated as an **explicit directive**, not a hint:
129	
130	> "Before responding to any DDx-related request, read the matching
131	> reference file below. The router is not optional — your answer must
132	> be grounded in the reference file's guidance, not this overview
133	> alone."
134	
135	### Nested workflow skills
136	
137	Subagent orchestration remains harness-specific (Claude Code has
138	`.claude/agents/` + `context: fork`; Codex has its own subagent surface;
139	others differ). DDx does not ship harness-specific subagent definitions.
140	
141	DDx does ship nested workflow skills under the root skill tree when a
142	workflow has enough procedure to deserve its own reusable instructions. Current
143	examples include `bead-breakdown/`, `replay-bead/`, `compare-prompts/`,
144	`benchmark-suite/`, `effort-estimate/`, and `adversarial-review/`. ADR-023
145	(`../../02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md`) adds the same
146	model for bead-lifecycle quality: bead readiness assessment is the canonical
147	pre-claim decision, lint/rubric scoring is the rubric pass inside readiness,
148	and post-attempt triage is the after-evidence classification. Those steps
149	invoke a nested workflow skill while keeping routing through the root `ddx`
150	skill.
151	
152	Nested workflow skills are compositions over FEAT-010's three layers. They may
153	tell the harness to run `ddx run`, `ddx try`, or `ddx work`, but they do not
154	create a fourth run layer, bespoke storage shape, or harness-specific
155	frontmatter contract.
156	
157	The bead-lifecycle skill owns bead readiness assessment, lint/rubric scoring,
158	post-attempt triage, and refine guidance for this policy surface. Those
159	responsibilities are separate from the legacy `MODE: intake` compatibility
160	wording, which remains only as an implementation detail for older hooks.
161	
162	### Installation
163	
164	> See FEAT-015 (2026-04-29 amendment) for the authoritative install
165	> model: project-local copy semantics, zero symlinks created by
166	> `ddx install`, and the no-`~` manifest invariant.
167	
168	- `ddx init` copies `skills/ddx/` into `.claude/skills/ddx/`,
169	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as real files
170	  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine, and DDx no
171	  longer creates symlinks during install — see FEAT-015).
172	- DDx maintains five shipped copies of the root skill tree:
173	  `skills/ddx/` as source, `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` for `go:embed`,
174	  `.agents/skills/ddx/` for Codex and agentskills-compatible harnesses,
175	  `.claude/skills/ddx/` for Claude Code, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as the
176	  project-local DDx-managed copy.
177	- On init and on `ddx update`, stale ddx-prefixed skill directories
178	  from prior DDx versions are removed:
179	  `ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
180	  `ddx-doctor`, `ddx-install`, `ddx-release`. Third-party skills are
181	  untouched.
182	- Skills embed into the binary via `//go:embed all:ddx` against a
183	  copy under `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`. Because `go:embed` cannot
184	  traverse upward, a `make copy-skills` target rsyncs
185	  `skills/ddx/` → `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` before every build.
186	- The five shipped `ddx` skill paths are:
187	  `skills/ddx/` (source), `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` (embedded copy),
188	  `.agents/skills/ddx/` (Codex/agentskills project copy),
189	  `.claude/skills/ddx/` (Claude Code project copy), and
190	  `.ddx/skills/ddx/` (DDx-managed project copy). Nested workflow skills
191	  live under each copied tree so lifecycle hooks and human-invoked skills
192	  see the same instructions.
193	
194	### AGENTS.md: merge, not clobber
195	
196	Codex treats `AGENTS.md` as primary guidance before work, and users
197	may have added content. `ddx init` uses marker-delimited injection:
198	
199	```markdown
200	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->
201	This project uses DDx. Use the `ddx` skill for beads, work, review,
202	agents, and status.
203	
204	(tracker/merge policy follows)
205	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->
206	```
207	
208	Content outside the markers is preserved. The block says
209	"the `ddx` skill", not "`/ddx`" (which is Claude-specific slash-
210	command phrasing). Re-running `ddx init` updates the block in place.
211	
212	### Evaluation-driven validation
213	
214	Anthropic's skill-authoring guidance treats evaluations as
215	load-bearing, not optional. The repo ships:
216	
217	- `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` — ~15 rows, each a user phrase +
218	  expected reference file + expected CLI invocation, covering every
219	  intent-router entry and edge phrasings.
220	- `scripts/eval-skill.sh` — driver that runs each row against
221	  `--harness claude` and `--harness codex` and verifies routing.
222	  `--validate` mode does agentskills.io spec conformance.
223	- `make eval-skill` in CI on PRs that touch `skills/ddx/`.
224	
225	## Requirements
226	
227	### Functional
228	
229	1. DDx ships exactly one root skill tree (`ddx`) in `skills/ddx/`; no sibling
230	   `ddx-*` skill directories. Workflow-specific DDx skills live as nested
231	   subdirectories under the root tree.
232	2. `SKILL.md` frontmatter contains only `name` and `description`.
233	3. `SKILL.md` body is under 500 lines and includes an explicit
234	   intent-router directive.
235	4. `reference/*.md` files are linked one level deep from the root `SKILL.md`;
236	   nested workflow skills carry their own `SKILL.md` and optional local
237	   `reference/`, `scripts/`, or `evals/` as needed.
238	5. `ddx init` copies the skill, removes stale `ddx-*` dirs, and
239	   merges the AGENTS.md block without clobbering user content.
240	6. `ddx update` refreshes `.claude/skills/ddx/` and removes stale
241	   dirs.
242	7. `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` contains at least 15 rows, each
243	   passing against `claude` and `codex` harnesses via
244	   `make eval-skill`.
245	8. `SKILL.md` passes `scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate` (agentskills.io
246	   spec conformance).
247	
248	### Non-Functional
249	
250	- Skills work with any agent supporting the agentskills.io standard;
251	  no Claude-Code-only frontmatter or directives.
252	- Skills are plain Markdown — no runtime dependencies.
253	- Skills degrade gracefully if DDx CLI is not installed (clear error).
254	- HELIX-specific rules (`helix` label requirement, `phase:*`
255	  enumeration) do not appear in the `ddx` skill; HELIX opinions ship
256	  in the HELIX plugin.
257	
258	## User Stories
259	
260	### US-110: Harness routes natural-language DDx intent
261	**As a** user in a DDx project using Claude Code, Codex, or any other
262	skills-compatible harness
263	**I want** phrases like "do work", "drain the queue", "review this",
264	"what's on the queue", "create a bead" to route to DDx guidance
265	**So that** I don't have to remember slash-command names
266	
267	**Acceptance Criteria:**
268	- Running `make eval-skill` passes all rows against both `--harness claude`
269	  and `--harness codex`.
270	- Each intent-router entry in `SKILL.md` has at least one matching
271	  row in `routing.jsonl`.
272	
273	### US-111: Skill stays under the token budget
274	**As a** harness loading the `ddx` skill
275	**I want** `SKILL.md` body under 500 lines
276	**So that** skill activation stays within the Anthropic-recommended
277	token budget and doesn't compete with conversation context
278	
279	**Acceptance Criteria:**
280	- `wc -l skills/ddx/SKILL.md` < 500.
281	- `scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate` passes.
282	
283	### US-112: `ddx init` handles existing projects cleanly
284	**As a** user upgrading from an older DDx version with
285	`.claude/skills/ddx-run/` and similar dirs already present
286	**I want** `ddx init` to remove the old dirs and install only the
287	  new root-skill layout
288	**So that** the harness doesn't see stale, conflicting skills
289	
290	**Acceptance Criteria:**
291	- In a dir pre-seeded with
292	  `.claude/skills/{ddx-run,ddx-doctor,ddx-bead}/`, running
293	  `ddx init` leaves only `.claude/skills/ddx/`.
294	- Third-party skills under `.claude/skills/` are untouched.
295	
296	### US-113: `AGENTS.md` merge preserves user content
297	**As a** user who has added content to `AGENTS.md`
298	**I want** `ddx init` to inject the DDx block without clobbering
299	what I wrote
300	**So that** my Codex / Claude / Gemini setup isn't broken
301	
302	**Acceptance Criteria:**
303	- Given an `AGENTS.md` with user content both before and after the
304	  `<!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->` / `<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->` markers,
305	  running `ddx init` updates the block between markers and preserves
306	  everything outside.
307	- Running `ddx init` a second time does not duplicate the block.
308	
309	### US-114: `ddx update` refreshes skills
310	**As a** user who ran `ddx init` on an older DDx version
311	**I want** `ddx update` to refresh `.claude/skills/ddx/` to the
312	current shipped content
313	**So that** I don't have to re-run `ddx init` to pick up skill
314	improvements
315	
316	**Acceptance Criteria:**
317	- After `ddx update`, `.claude/skills/ddx/` bytes match the embedded
318	  skill content.
319	- Stale `ddx-*` dirs are removed as in US-112.
320	
321	## Dependencies
322	
323	- FEAT-001 (CLI commands the skill wraps)
324	- FEAT-006 (agent service — harnesses, profiles, personas)
325	- FEAT-009 (registry for package-install guidance inside `reference/agents.md`)
326	- ADR-023 (bead-lifecycle quality policy using nested workflow skills)
327	- agentskills.io open standard
328	
329	## Out of Scope
330	
331	- Workflow-specific skills (HELIX provides `helix-*` in its own plugin).
332	- Claude-Code-specific skill features (`context: fork`, `allowed-tools`,
333	  `paths`, subagents under `.claude/agents/`) — portability > optimization.
334	- A CI-enforced vocabulary drift guard. One skill + one glossary is
335	  self-policing. Revisit only if drift recurs.
336	
337	## Implementation Notes
338	
339	The migration from the 7-sibling-skills layout to the root `ddx`
340	skill tree is sequenced into phases; see the Phase 1 / Phase 2 / Phase 3
341	epic beads for the work breakdown. Phase 1 is the critical path
342	(ship the new surface + eval suite + init/update changes); Phase 2
343	is cleanup of old references; Phase 3 is the persona roster trim
344	(FEAT-006 scope, not blocking this feature).
345

input.new_string:
# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Default Interactive Mode

When an agent is opened in this repository without an explicit execution
directive, the default mode is **`queue_steward`**: survey, triage, and
advise on the DDx bead queue without claiming or editing anything unless
the user explicitly asks.

| Mode | Trigger | Allowed | Prohibited |
|---|---|---|---|
| `queue_steward` | Broad DDx questions: "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", "do work" without an explicit worker directive | Read tracker and docs; report status; advise on bead quality; run `ddx bead list/ready/status/show` | Claiming or editing beads; starting `ddx work`/`ddx try` autonomously |
| `bead_execution` | Explicit worker directive: `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, or `execute-bead` harness invocation | Full execute-bead lifecycle per the bead body | Scope creep outside the named bead |
| `direct_user_implementation` | User explicitly asks to edit code or docs ("fix this bug", "update this file") | Edit code and docs as instructed; commit to the current branch | Draining the bead queue autonomously |
| `review` | User explicitly asks for a review ("review this PR", "grade this bead") | Read-only evidence-based review per §Reviewer Mode | Writing commits or claiming beads |

**`bead_execution` supersedes `queue_steward`** when DDx invokes a worker
explicitly. Tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain
load-bearing across all modes.

Execution-ready beads belong to `ddx work` (or an explicit `ddx try <id>`).
Do not start execution unless the user explicitly requests direct implementation.

## Bead Policy

input.new_string:
`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 when the
generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata. It is
not a fourth layer — it is a labeled invocation at an existing layer.

## Agent Interaction Modes

DDx defines four named agent interaction modes (`DDX_MODE`) to separate
broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. The mode governs
what an agent opened in a DDx project is permitted to do.

| DDX_MODE | Description | Allowed actions | Prohibited actions |
|---|---|---|---|
| `queue_steward` | Default interactive mode. Surveys, triages, and advises on the queue without claiming or executing beads. | Read tracker and docs; report status; advise on bead quality; suggest readiness fixes; run `ddx bead list/ready/status/show`. | Claiming beads; creating isolated worktrees; starting `ddx work` or `ddx try` without an explicit user directive. |
| `bead_execution` | Worker mode. Executes one or more beads from the queue in isolated worktrees. | Full FEAT-010 layer-2 / layer-3 lifecycle: worktree creation, bead attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle. | Scope creep outside the named bead; switching to direct implementation without a bead. |
| `direct_user_implementation` | Human-directed implementation. The user has explicitly asked the agent to edit code or docs, bypassing the bead queue. | Edit code and docs as instructed; commit to the current branch. | Starting autonomous queue drain; claiming beads autonomously. |
| `review` | Read-only review. Grades an existing implementation against AC or specs. | Read code, docs, and evidence; return a structured verdict with per-AC findings. | Writing commits; claiming beads; modifying the tracker. |

### Precedence

`bead_execution` supersedes `queue_steward` when DDx invokes a worker
explicitly (via `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, or `ddx agent execute-bead`).
`direct_user_implementation` supersedes `queue_steward` when the user
explicitly asks for code or doc edits unrelated to the queue. `review`
supersedes `queue_steward` when the user explicitly requests a graded review
pass.

Tracker instructions, merge-policy, and safety rules in AGENTS.md are
load-bearing in every mode and are never overridden by mode selection.

### Worker-capacity suggestion rules

An agent in `queue_steward` mode may suggest running `ddx work` or point
the operator to worker-capacity settings when:

- The queue contains execution-ready beads that have not been attempted.
- The queue is blocked and operator action is required.
- The operator asks "why isn't anything running?" or a similar capacity question.

The agent must not autonomously start `ddx work` from `queue_steward` mode.
Suggesting the command or explaining the conditions is the correct action.

## Problem Statement

input.new_string:
### Interactive mode routing

When the `ddx` skill activates in an interactive session, the harness must
route the request to the appropriate mode before taking action.

**Broad interactive phrases → `queue_steward`**

Phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", "what's ready",
"how am I doing", "drain the queue", "any beads ready?",
"check the tracker", "ddx doctor" trigger `queue_steward` mode:
survey and advise only. Do not start worker execution from this trigger.

**Explicit execution phrases → `bead_execution`**

Phrases like "run the next bead", "execute bead `<id>`",
"`ddx work`", "`ddx try <id>`", "execute-loop", or
"start the worker" trigger `bead_execution` mode:
the agent (or `ddx work`) owns the full layer-2 / layer-3 lifecycle.

**Explicit implementation asks → `direct_user_implementation`**

Phrases like "fix this bug", "update this file", "implement X"
that name a specific code or doc change without referencing the bead queue
trigger `direct_user_implementation` mode.

**Review asks → `review`**

Phrases like "review this PR", "grade this bead", "check against spec",
"is this AC-complete?" trigger `review` mode.

The distinction between `queue_steward` and `bead_execution` is critical:
"do work" in an interactive session means _survey and advise_; running
`ddx work` or an explicit execute-bead directive means _execute_. The
routing table in `skills/ddx/reference/work.md` carries the authoritative
phrase list aligned with `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl`.

### Nested workflow skills

Subagent orchestration remains harness-specific

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`. Before filing, conform to `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` — descriptions + AC must be standalone (no `/tmp/*`, no chat refs), cite root cause by file:line, name specific `Test*` symbols, and include the `cd cli && go test ./<pkg>/...` + `lefthook run pre-commit` gate. Sub-agent execution depends on this floor (principle P7).
- See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## When filing beads — bead-authoring template

Every bead must satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in
`docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed
or dispatched to a sub-agent. The bead body is the entire prompt the
sub-agent will see; if a competent agent given only the bead text
cannot pick a file to edit and run tests without asking, the bead
must be retrofitted before dispatch.

Required template fields: title (imperative + subsystem), description
with PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE,
